location: After '## What This Does' paragraph, before '## Up-Front Questionnaire'
content:
## Quick Reference

- **Gate:** candidate must beat the best by more than gate_margin (strict; ties rejected)
- **Budget:** ≤max_ops ops, net |added−removed| ≤ max_words
- **Frozen target:** each subagent sees only skill text + task prompt
rationale: Place key terms (beat, best, candidate, subagent, skill, task) at the TOP where subagents see them first. Iter-01 showed that placing 'beat the best' inside the defaults table doesn't anchor terminology strongly enough — subagents paraphrase it away. A top-level Quick Reference section will be the first thing read and should anchor concise answers.

content:
## ledger.py CLI Reference

```
ledger.py record --run <path> --iter <N> --version <label> --split train|holdout --scores <comma-separated-floats>
ledger.py gate   --run <path> --iter <N> --candidate <cN> --margin <float>
ledger.py best   --run <path>
```

`--scores` takes comma-separated floats, e.g. `--scores "0.8,1.0,0.6"`.
rationale: Same as iter-01 — subagents guess --run-dir and space-separated scores. Still needed.

location: Minibatch Sampling section, after last bullet
content: Epoch count after K iterations = K×M/T (float division; partial epochs count).
rationale: Same as iter-01 — subagent used integer division. Still needed.
